transcript.text: What does this project do and who uses it?

It's a Node/TypeScript SDK that fetches and renders codegen scaffolding templates from our private template registry. Used by our own internal CLI tools — not distributed to external customers, and never loaded via `<script src>` in a browser.

What's the distribution mechanism here — package.json has no `browser` field and no bundler config pointing at a CDN artifact, so is this ever bundled and shipped straight into a browser page, or does it stay server/CLI-side only?
It's an npm-installed library that runs inside internal Node CLI tooling — node-server style, not browser-direct-js. It's never bundled into a page.

What's the audience, and — I see a `DEFAULT_API_KEY` fallback and a `RegistryClientOptions.apiKey` field in registryClient.ts — is that a real production credential or a throwaway local-dev convenience?
Internal only — our own developer tooling team. No PII or payment data. That API key is a real credential though, so treat it as sensitive.
Any compliance or regulatory requirements I should know about?
No. Internal tooling — no SOC2, HIPAA, or PCI requirement.
Is this project AI-powered, or built with / used by LLM coding agents?
It's built with Claude Code day to day, but the SDK itself doesn't call an LLM at runtime and isn't invoked by one — it's a plain template-fetching client library.
I see two minified files under vendor/ — qs-parse.min.js has a THIRD_PARTY.md entry but format-utils.min.js doesn't. Was that intentional, or did the provenance note get missed?
That was missed — format-utils.min.js should have gotten a THIRD_PARTY.md entry when it was vendored in. Please flag it; we'll backfill the entry.
What threshold should gate-6 enforce for this package?
80% is fine, same as our other internal packages.

exceedsBaseline.note: Zero runtime dependencies — the two vendored files in vendor/ replace what would otherwise be two npm dependencies, keeping the declared dependency tree empty (this strength is independent of, and does not excuse, the vendored-file provenance gap also reported on gate-1)

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
  rel="${f#$root/}"
  base="$(basename "$f")"
  if ! grep -q "$base" "$root/THIRD_PARTY.md" 2>/dev/null; then
    out+="$rel:1"$'\t'"vendored file missing THIRD_PARTY.md provenance entry"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  fi
done < <(find "$root/vendor" -type f -name '*.min.js' -print0 2>/dev/null)
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS=: read -r file line rest; do
  out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"public export throws \"not implemented\" (stub implementation)"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(grep -rn --include='*.ts' -B2 'throw new Error("not implemented")' "$root/src" 2>/dev/null \
  | awk -F- '/not implemented/{print}' | grep -oE '^[^:]+:[0-9]+' )
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
# semgrep run against custom rules for hardcoded-secret / shell-injection /
# eval-in-comment; output normalized to file:line\tmessage below.
results="$(semgrep --config "$root/.karen/rules/security.yml" --json "$root/src" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"results":[]}')"
issues=0
out=""
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(echo "$results" | node "$root/.karen/gates/format-semgrep.js")
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-4-docs-parity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
node "$root/tools/doctest.mjs" --root "$root" --files README.md --lang ts,typescript --annotation karen:runnable || issues=$?
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
cd "$root"
npm test --silent
coverage="$(node -e "console.log(require('./coverage/coverage-summary.json').total.lines.pct)" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
threshold=80
if awk "BEGIN{exit !($coverage >= $threshold)}"; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0
else
  echo "coverage/lcov.info:1"$'\t'"coverage $coverage% below threshold $threshold%"
  echo "FAIL (1 issues)"; exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
if [ ! -f "$root/CLAUDE.md" ]; then
  out+="CLAUDE.md"$'\t'"missing agent context file"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
